user_message: Give me the manager view on this Pacific Northwest Outfitters escalation email. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: partner engagement Discussion - Pacific Northwest Outfitters
Date: 2024-01-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Pacific Northwest Outfitters)

[00:01:15] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is partner engagement.

[00:08:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Andrew Taylor (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): The timeline for technical validation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:18:00] Michelle (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:23:00] Michelle (Pacific Northwest Outfitters): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:27:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): On the partner engagement front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-M trap (Partner-evaluation module assumes single-partner when workshop involved multiple); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-M failure mode (Partner-evaluation module assumes single-partner when workshop involved multiple)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct partner engagement decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-M -- Partner-evaluation module assumes single-partner when workshop involved multiple
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
